content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with all atom labels visible. Let me examine atom C:21 (carbon with index 21) and identify its neighboring atoms by looking at the direct bonds.

From the image, I can clearly see atom C:21 is connected to:
- C:20 (carbon, index 20) - directly bonded
- C:22 (carbon, index 22) - directly bonded  
- H:32 (hydrogen, index 32) - directly bonded

These three atoms are covalently connected to C:21. The atom C:21 appears to be part of a ring structure and has three covalent bonds total.

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 8968, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_8968.xyz".

Let me confirm once more by checking if there are any other connections. Looking at the molecular structure, C:21 is in a ring system and has exactly three bonds: two to carbon atoms (C:20 and C:22) and one to hydrogen (H:32).

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom 21 (C:21) are:
- Atom index 20, element C
- Atom index 22, element C  
- Atom index 32, element H

Geomconf_index: 8968

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 21, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
